steps.title: Identify Putirka and Xu's core criticism
steps.content: The key signal is Putirka and Xu's argument that the detected elements could come from multiple sources beyond Earth-like continental crusts—Holland's conclusion doesn't adequately consider these alternatives.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the characterization
steps.content: Holland's conclusion assumes the detected elements point exclusively to Earth-like continental crusts, but this reasoning is flawed because it doesn't account for alternative sources—making it As questionable, because it rests on an incomplete consideration of potential sources of the elements detected in white dwarf atmospheres is correct.
